pr.title: Match/improve 52 functions across 16 TUs (1 at 100%, 35 at >=90%)
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Large decomp-matching batch that replaced placeholders with C source for 52 functions across 16 translation units, with one 100% match and 35 functions at or above 90%. The work advanced Kirby copy-ability setup/specials, CPU attack code, camera and game-mode code, multiple gr/* stage files, trophy/toy list code, lbarq, and sysdolphin baselib functions. The PR also tightened several prototypes and headers to satisfy strict CI flags. A review-cycle issue around toy.c layout regressions was fixed by reverting toy.c to upstream shape; remaining tiny regressions were explained as sdata2/literal-pool displacement artifacts from adding new bodies into existing TUs.
what_changed: Added source for 52 formerly placeholder functions across 16 translation units.

Matched or improved functions in Kirby copy-ability code, including ftKb_SpecialN_800EEC34, ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F16D0.
Added four functions in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c for Kirby/Yoshi special-N behavior, including two 99.90% matches.
Added CPU attack source for ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800BB220.
Added game-mode and camera bodies including gm_80164910 and four gmCamera functions.
Added or improved stage and ground code in grfourside.c, grkongo.c, grmaterial.c, ground.c, grpura.c, grvenom.c, and gricemt.c.
Added trophy/toy-list source in tylist.c and toy-related header/prototype updates.
Added library and baselib source including lbArq_80014BD0, HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress, and six functions in hsd_3AA7.c.
Updated prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where evidence was available, such as fn_800DA8E4 to void fn_800DA8E4(Fighter_GObj* arg0, Fighter_GObj* arg1, s32 arg2) and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438 to void.
Aligned grPura_80213030 signature to take Ground_GObj* instead of void, according to the PR notes.
Added prototypes in tylist.h for un_80312BAC, un_8031305C, fn_80313BD8, and fn_8031438C.

Added includes surfaced by strict CI/prototype requirements, including ft/ftmaterial.h, lb/lbarchive.h, lb/lbdvd.h, lb/lbvector.h, ft/ftlib.h, item headers, gr/grmaterial.h, baselib/debug.h, lb/lbaudio_ax.h, mn/mnmain.h, and baselib/id.h.
An earlier ftCo_0A01.c portion was reverted because adding 16 bodies there degraded existing high-quality matches; those functions were deferred for a separate approach.
A toy.c regression caused by local structural/layout divergence was fixed by reverting toy.c to upstream entirely.
smart_moves: The author separated useful but imperfect source bodies from match-quality claims by reporting percent tiers and explicitly flagging below-80% functions as permuter starting points rather than finished matches.
The PR used strict CI validation with build-linux/GALE01/main.dol OK under --max-errors 0 --require-protos --map, which exposed missing prototypes/includes and helped constrain header updates.
When ftCo_0A01.c additions damaged existing 100% and near-match functions, the author reverted that part instead of accepting broad regressions, preserving upstream match quality.

The review-cycle toy.c fix identified that data-section layout, not the unchanged function body, caused un_80309338 to drop from 100% to 97.65%; reverting toy.c to upstream shape restored the match and cleared neighboring regressions.
The author distinguished serious layout divergence from expected small sdata2/literal-pool displacement artifacts caused by adding new function bodies into a TU.

The PR documented examples of harmless byte-level regressions in grkongo.c, gricemt.c, grvenom.c, grpura.c, grfourside.c, and ftcpuattack.c where existing function bodies were unchanged but offsets into local data changed.

Kirby hat/copy-ability decomp used existing engine APIs and structures such as GET_FIGHTER, ftPartsPObjSetDefaultClass, HSD_IDInsertToTable, HSD_DObjLoadDesc, HSD_DObjResolveRefsAll, HSD_JObjAddDObj, lb_8000CE30, hsdChangeClass, and ftParts_8007487C rather than opaque stubs.
The author added a local tools/verify_pr.py workflow note to catch upstream-baseline regressions before future pushes, though the tool itself was not included in the PR evidence.
decomp_lessons: Adding C bodies to previously placeholder functions can shift a TU's string/float literal pools and sdata2 offsets; unchanged neighboring functions may lose match percentage solely from different 16-bit displacement operands.

Local file divergence such as struct redefinitions, include reordering, and new externs can move data sections enough to break a 100% match even if the function body is byte-for-byte equivalent in source shape.
Before landing large batch decomp PRs, compare against upstream/master to distinguish true functional regressions from layout artifacts and to catch accidental local-only file drift.

For files with existing high-quality matches, avoid large layout-altering additions unless the net match impact is acceptable; otherwise split into a separate PR, use NON_MATCHING gating, or seek permuter-friendly variants.
Strict prototype enforcement is useful during decomp batches because it forces real signatures and include dependencies to be made explicit.
Header changes should be tied to observed definitions or bodies; examples in this PR include replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and aligning argument lists where source bodies established types.
When a function is structurally correct but blocked by mwcc register allocation or scheduling, landing it can be useful as a future permuter seed if reviewers accept the tradeoff.
Large multi-TU batches should include a tier breakdown and a regression explanation so reviewers can judge tradeoffs across new matches versus layout-induced drops.
naming_conventions: Address-based names remained in use for unidentified functions, such as ftKb_SpecialN_800EEC34, gm_80164910, grVenom_80205758, un_8031305C, fn_80313BD8, and hsd_803B2374.
Static or local unidentified functions used the fn_ prefix plus address pattern, for example fn_801F3F74, fn_801C8EF8, fn_80313BD8, and fn_8031438C.

Known subsystem prefixes were preserved: ftKb_ for Kirby fighter code, ftCo_ for common fighter code, gmCamera_ for camera code, grKongo_/grFourside_/grVenom_/grPura_ for stage code, Ground_ for ground code, lbArq_ for library ARQ code, and HSD_ or hsd_ for baselib.
Header prototypes were improved from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete types only where the PR had enough evidence from definitions or upstream bodies.
The PR notes retained s32 for un_8030813C's argument type because it matched the body in toy.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used percent-tier reporting for every added function to make match quality searchable and reviewable.
Accepted some below-80% bodies as structurally correct C sources when remaining mismatch was attributed to mwcc regalloc or scheduling.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0, indicating inlining control was needed for matching or stability.
Used PAD_STACK in Kirby functions where stack layout mattered, as shown in ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0.
Preserved explicit pointer arithmetic and byte offsets in FighterBone and DObj-list traversal to mirror existing layout-sensitive assembly.
Tracked sdata2 and literal-pool reorder effects as acceptable small regressions when only load-displacement operands changed.
Reverted toy.c to upstream file shape to restore data-section layout and eliminate a 100% match regression.
Deferred ftCo_0A01.c additions because they shifted layout enough to break or severely degrade existing matches, suggesting a need for NON_MATCHING gating or different permuter variants.
review_feedback: There were no review comments in the provided excerpt, but the author replied to a review by pushing commit 85d5a32.
The review-cycle response fixed the broken un_80309338 match, which had regressed from 100% to 97.65%.

The stated root cause for the un_80309338 regression was toy.c diverging from upstream by 177 lines due to struct redefinitions, include reorder, and new externs, shifting toy.o's data section by about 1.3KB.
Reverting toy.c to upstream also cleared seven sub-percent neighboring regressions: un_80306EEC, un_803084A0, un_80308F04, un_80310324, un_8030715C, un_80312050, and Trophy_SetUnlockState.
The author disclosed about 24 remaining unmatched-tier regressions in other TUs, all under 0.15%, and characterized them as expected sdata2/literal-pool reorder artifacts.
The author offered to revert additional new bodies if reviewers preferred a cleaner regression report, but the PR was merged with the tradeoff.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that reverted or split decomp additions because adding bodies shifted TU layout and degraded existing matches.
Find examples of sdata2 or literal-pool reorder artifacts after replacing placeholders with C source.
Find Kirby copy-ability decomp work involving ftKb_Init.c and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4.
Find PRs that changed UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS prototypes to concrete Fighter_GObj signatures.
Find uses of #pragma dont_inline and PAD_STACK as matching tactics in fighter code.
Find future work on the ftCo_0A01.c functions deferred from PR 2469.
Find PRs mentioning tools/verify_pr.py or upstream/master baseline regression checks.
Find trophy/toy layout regression fixes around toy.c and un_80309338.
